title: Replacement Cycling
description: Replacement cycling repeatedly uses RBF and CPFP to evict a time-sensitive multiparty transaction from the mempool, preventing its confirmation while the attacker can afford to keep cycling replacements.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech: Replacement Cycling
resources.notes: Explains the repeated RBF-and-CPFP eviction attack and its impact on contract protocols.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech: Transaction Pinning
resources.notes: Context for the wider family of fee-bumping and confirmation-blocking attacks.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Background on fee bumping, transaction packages, and mempool policy.
